classical_node_name: VMHvl estrogen-receptor alpha / progesterone receptor neuron

0.name: 2290 VMH Fezf1 Glut_1
0.rationale: VMH location (correct region); glutamatergic (expected for VMHvl); strongly female-biased (ratio 0.08 — females 12x more abundant than males), which matches the female-biased dimorphism of VMHvl Esr1/Pgr neurons; parent SUPT_0563 (VMH Fezf1 Glut_1). Score-4 hit. Top rank-0 candidate.

0.name: 2292 VMH Fezf1 Glut_1
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; strongly female-biased (ratio 0.12 — ~8x more females), consistent with female-biased lordosis population. Score-4 hit. Sister cluster to 2290 under SUPT_0563; together these two are the best sex-ratio matched VMH clusters.

0.name: 2295 VMH Fezf1 Glut_2
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; score-4 marker match. However, male_female_ratio = 1.44 (slightly male-biased), which does not align with the female-biased dimorphism expected for the lordosis-mediating Esr1/Pgr population. Could represent a VMHvl subtype with a different function (e.g., aggression-related). Note: given 17 transcriptomic subtypes, some VMHvl clusters will be male-biased even within the Esr1+ parent group.

0.name: 2297 VMH Fezf1 Glut_2
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; score-4. Male_female_ratio = 1.44 (slightly male-biased). Same caveats as 2295 — plausible VMHvl subtype but sex ratio direction is inconsistent with female-biased lordosis function. May map to the Pgr+ aggression-fighting subtypes rather than the female locomotion/lordosis subtype.

0.name: 2296 VMH Fezf1 Glut_2
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; score-3. Male_female_ratio = 1.94 (nearly 2x male-biased). Mismatch with female-biased dimorphism, but given the 17-subtype heterogeneity of VMHvl, this could represent a functionally distinct male-type VMHvl subtype. Lower priority than 2290/2292.

0.name: 2294 VMH Fezf1 Glut_2
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; score-3. Male_female_ratio = 1.94. Same caveats as 2296 — VMH region is correct but sex ratio direction is inconsistent with the female-biased Esr1/Pgr lordosis population. Possible VMHvl aggression/fighting subtype.

0.name: 2300 VMH Fezf1 Glut_3
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; score-3. Male_female_ratio = 0.92 (near-equal). Neutral sex ratio is consistent with a VMHvl population not strongly dimorphic in cell number. Could correspond to one of the 17 transcriptomic subtypes without strong sex bias.

0.name: 2299 VMH Fezf1 Glut_3
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; score-3. Male_female_ratio = 3.17 (strongly male-biased). Sex ratio direction is inconsistent with the female-biased Esr1/Pgr neuron, but VMHvl contains subtypes regulating male aggression/fighting — this may map to one of those. Lower priority for the lordosis/female-locomotion subtype.

0.name: 2291 VMH Fezf1 Glut_1
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; score-3. Male_female_ratio = 1.27 (slight male bias). Same parent (SUPT_0563) as the female-biased 2290/2292. Slight male bias may indicate this cluster captures a different VMHvl subdivision or functional subtype.

0.name: 2301 VMH Nr5a1 Glut_1
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; Nr5a1 (SF-1/steroidogenic factor 1) is a canonical VMH transcription factor; score-3. Male_female_ratio = 0.96 (near-equal). Nr5a1+ VMHvl neurons overlap with Esr1 circuitry. Reasonable VMHvl candidate; sex ratio neutral.

0.name: 2307 VMH Nr5a1 Glut_2
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; Nr5a1+; score-3. Male_female_ratio = 3.55 (strongly male-biased). More consistent with male-type VMHvl function (aggression) than the female-biased Esr1/Pgr/lordosis population. Lower priority.

0.name: 2313 VMH Nr5a1 Glut_3
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; Nr5a1+; score-3. Male_female_ratio = 1.94. Slight male bias; canonical VMH marker. Lower priority than female-biased candidates but VMH region is correct.

0.name: 2315 VMH Nr5a1 Glut_4
0.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; Nr5a1+; score-3. Male_female_ratio = 1.7. Slight male bias. Canonical VMH marker; plausible but not the best sex-ratio match for the female-biased Esr1/Pgr population.

0.name: 1577 TMv-PMv Tbx3 Hist-Gaba_1
0.rationale: TMv-PMv (tuberomammillary/premammillary) is a different hypothalamic region, not VMH. Histaminergic/GABAergic NT type is inconsistent with glutamatergic VMHvl. Location is wrong. Score-3 reflects marker overlap only.

0.name: 1810 DMH-LHA Gsx1 Gaba_3
0.rationale: DMH-LHA (dorsomedial hypothalamus / lateral hypothalamic area) — wrong region. Cholinergic NT type is inconsistent with VMHvl glutamatergic profile. Location disqualifies this candidate.

0.rationale: TU-ARH (tuberal area / arcuate nucleus) — wrong region. GABAergic. Marker score may reflect Six6 or Otp overlap. Location disqualifies.

1.name: 0563 VMH Fezf1 Glut_1
1.rationale: IMPORTANT: This supertype was NOT retrieved in the rank-1 query but is the direct parent of the two top STRONG rank-0 candidates (CLUS_2290 and CLUS_2292), which are strongly female-biased (ratios 0.08 and 0.12). VMH Fezf1 Glut_1 is a VMH glutamatergic supertype — correct region, correct NT type, correct sex bias direction. Its absence from the rank-1 results is a gap in the DB query. It should be treated as the top rank-1 candidate by hierarchy. Flagged for curator review.

1.name: 0564 VMH Fezf1 Glut_2
1.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; score-2 at rank-1. Parent of CLUS_2295 and 2297 (both score-4 rank-0 candidates). VMH Fezf1 Glut_2 supertype encompasses multiple VMH clusters including some female-biased (2295/2297 ratio 1.44 — slight male, but the Fezf1 Glut_2 grouping contains multiple subtypes). Strong location match; top retrieved rank-1 VMH supertype.

1.name: 0565 VMH Fezf1 Glut_3
1.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; score-2. Parent of CLUS_2299 (ratio 3.17, male-biased) and CLUS_2300 (ratio 0.92, neutral). VMH Fezf1 supertype — correct region. Sex ratio within this supertype is mixed, suggesting it spans multiple VMHvl functional subtypes. Plausible, lower priority than 0563/0564.

1.name: 0566 VMH Nr5a1 Glut_1
1.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; Nr5a1 (SF-1) is the canonical VMH transcription factor and is strongly co-expressed with Esr1 in VMHvl; score-2. Contains CLUS_2301 (ratio 0.96, near-equal). Plausible VMHvl supertype.

1.name: 0567 VMH Nr5a1 Glut_2
1.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; Nr5a1+; score-2. Contains CLUS_2307 (ratio 3.55, male-biased). Correct region but sex ratio direction is inconsistent with female-biased Esr1/Pgr population. Could represent male-type VMHvl subtypes.

1.name: 0568 VMH Nr5a1 Glut_3
1.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; Nr5a1+; score-2. Contains CLUS_2313 (ratio 1.94). Correct region, plausible VMHvl supertype.

1.name: 0569 VMH Nr5a1 Glut_4
1.rationale: VMH location; glutamatergic; Nr5a1+; score-2. Contains CLUS_2315 (ratio 1.7). Correct region, plausible VMHvl supertype.

non_candidates_summary: The DB-scoring function correctly elevated VMH clusters to the top positions. The key discriminators at rank-0 are: (1) VMH label in cluster name (correct region); (2) Glut NT type (expected for VMHvl); (3) sex ratio — strongly female-biased clusters (2290: 0.08, 2292: 0.12) are the best sex ratio match for the lordosis/locomotion Esr1+Pgr+Nkx2-1+Tac1 population, while male-biased VMH clusters (2299: 3.17, 2307: 3.55) likely correspond to the aggression/fighting subtypes also known to be in VMHvl. All non-VMH candidates (DMH, ARH-PVp, TU-ARH, TMv-PMv, LHA) are WEAK or EXCLUDE on location grounds despite scoring 3 at the DB level — the marker score partially reflects hormone receptor co-expression across the hypothalamus. CRITICAL GAP: SUPT_0563 (VMH Fezf1 Glut_1, parent of the best two rank-0 candidates) was not retrieved in the rank-1 query; this is a query-coverage issue. Given the 17-subtype heterogeneity of vmhvl_esr1_pr_neuron, the expected mapping is a CROSS-CUTTING relationship across multiple VMH supertypes (0563, 0564, 0565, 0566-0569) — a single best match is unlikely, and curator should treat the full VMH Fezf1/Nr5a1 Glut supertype set as collectively spanning the classical node.
